title: 1. Which is the largest continent in the world?
summary: Asia is the largest continent by both area and population.

title: 2. Which is the longest river in the world?
summary: The Nile River is traditionally regarded as the longest river in the world.

title: 3. Which is the largest ocean on Earth?
summary: The Pacific Ocean covers more area than all other oceans.

title: 4. Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
summary: Mars appears reddish due to iron oxide on its surface.

title: 5. Which is the highest mountain peak in the world?
summary: Mount Everest is the highest peak above sea level.

title: 6. Which is the largest desert in the world?
summary: Antarctica is technically the largest desert due to extremely low precipitation.

title: 7. What is the capital of Australia?
summary: Canberra was selected as a compromise between Sydney and Melbourne.

title: 8. Which is the largest state in India by area?
summary: Rajasthan is the largest Indian state by geographical area.

title: 9. Which river is known as the 'Ganga of the South'?
summary: The Godavari is often called the Ganga of South India.

title: 10. Which layer of Earth do we live on?
summary: Humans live on Earth's outermost solid layer called the crust.

title: 11. Which latitude is known as the Tropic of Cancer?
summary: The Tropic of Cancer lies at 23.5° North latitude.

title: 12. Which is the deepest ocean trench in the world?
summary: The Mariana Trench contains the deepest known point in Earth's oceans.

title: 13. Which Indian state has the longest coastline?
summary: Gujarat has the longest coastline among Indian states.

title: 14. Which river originates from Gangotri Glacier?
summary: The Bhagirathi River originates from the Gangotri Glacier.

title: 15. Which type of rainfall is common in equatorial regions?
summary: Convectional rainfall occurs due to intense heating and rising air.

title: 16. Which is the largest freshwater lake in India?
summary: Wular Lake in Jammu and Kashmir is India's largest freshwater lake.

title: 17. Which country is known as the Land of Rising Sun?
summary: Japan is known as the Land of Rising Sun.

title: 18. Which strait separates Asia and North America?
summary: The Bering Strait separates Russia and Alaska.

title: 19. Which Indian city is known as the Silicon Valley of India?
summary: Bengaluru is India's leading IT hub.

title: 20. Which plateau is called the Mineral Heartland of India?
summary: The Chota Nagpur Plateau is rich in minerals like coal and iron ore.

title: 21. Which pass connects Leh with Srinagar?
summary: Zoji La is a strategic mountain pass in the Himalayas.

title: 22. Which current ocean is named after a country?
summary: The Indian Ocean is the only ocean named after a country.

title: 23. Which is the only floating national park in the world?
summary: Keibul Lamjao National Park in Manipur is the world's only floating national park.

title: 24. Which river forms the world's largest delta?
summary: The Ganga-Brahmaputra river system forms the Sundarbans Delta.

title: 25. Which Indian state has the highest forest cover in terms of area?
summary: Madhya Pradesh has the largest forest area among Indian states.

title: 26. Which soil type is most suitable for cotton cultivation in India?
summary: Black soil, also known as Regur soil, retains moisture and is ideal for cotton cultivation.

title: 27. Which winds bring rainfall to most parts of India?
summary: The Southwest Monsoon accounts for nearly 75% of India's annual rainfall.

title: 28. Which is the largest coral reef system in the world?
summary: The Great Barrier Reef off Australia's coast is the world's largest coral reef system.

title: 29. Which river is known as the 'Sorrow of Bihar'?
summary: The Kosi River frequently changes course and causes devastating floods in Bihar.

title: 30. Which Indian state produces the highest amount of tea?
summary: Assam is India's leading tea-producing state.

title: 31. Which layer of the atmosphere contains the ozone layer?
summary: The ozone layer is concentrated in the stratosphere and absorbs harmful UV radiation.

title: 32. Which Indian river cuts through the Himalayas forming deep gorges?
summary: The Indus River is antecedent and cuts through the Himalayas forming deep valleys.

title: 33. Which type of volcano is Mount Fuji?
summary: Mount Fuji is a stratovolcano composed of alternating lava and ash layers.

title: 34. Which current is a warm ocean current?
summary: The Gulf Stream carries warm water from the Gulf of Mexico across the Atlantic.

title: 35. Which Indian state has the highest percentage of forest cover relative to its area?
summary: Mizoram consistently records one of the highest percentages of forest cover in India.

title: 36. Which river flows through a rift valley in India?
summary: The Narmada River flows westward through a rift valley between the Vindhya and Satpura ranges.

title: 37. What is the main cause of tides?
summary: Tides are primarily caused by the gravitational pull of the Moon.

title: 38. Which biome is characterized by permafrost?
summary: The tundra biome contains permanently frozen subsoil called permafrost.

title: 39. Which atmospheric phenomenon is associated with warming of eastern Pacific waters?
summary: El Niño affects global weather patterns and weakens monsoons in many years.

title: 40. Which plateau is formed mainly by basaltic lava flows?
summary: The Deccan Plateau originated from extensive volcanic eruptions known as the Deccan Traps.

title: 41. Which mountain range separates Europe and Asia?
summary: The Ural Mountains are conventionally considered the boundary between Europe and Asia.

title: 42. Which Indian biosphere reserve is shared with Nepal?
summary: The Terai Arc landscape includes important habitats across India and Nepal.

title: 43. Which is the largest inland drainage basin in India?
summary: The Luni Basin represents the largest inland drainage system in India.

title: 44. Which cloud type is associated with thunderstorms?
summary: Cumulonimbus clouds are towering clouds that produce thunderstorms and heavy rainfall.

title: 45. Which Indian state receives rainfall from both southwest and northeast monsoons?
summary: Tamil Nadu receives rainfall from both monsoon systems, especially the northeast monsoon.

title: 46. Which sea is the saltiest major sea in the world?
summary: The Red Sea experiences high evaporation and limited freshwater inflow, resulting in very high salinity.

title: 47. Which Indian city is located closest to the Tropic of Cancer?
summary: The Tropic of Cancer passes near Ujjain in Madhya Pradesh.

title: 48. Which country has the most active volcanoes?
summary: Indonesia lies on the Pacific Ring of Fire and has the highest number of active volcanoes.

title: 49. Which river is known as the Yellow River?
summary: The Huang He River carries large quantities of yellow silt from the Loess Plateau.

title: 50. Which line joins places having equal atmospheric pressure?
summary: Isobars are lines drawn on weather maps connecting points of equal pressure.
